Credentials

LinkedIn learning highlights (2025)

This is a simple record of the courses and certifications I shared publicly while building my career.

Home / Journal / Credentials / LinkedIn Learning Highlights
Credentials Published February 27, 2026 By Thomas Hayford

In 2025, I shared multiple learning milestones on LinkedIn. Most of them focused on spatial analysis, software foundations, and mobile product understanding. My goal was practical: improve how I support businesses and communicate with technical teams.

  • Esri Spatial Data Science (6-week intensive)Completed and shared with a week-by-week breakdown of business use cases.
  • ArcGIS Pro Basics (Esri)Posted as a certification milestone.
  • Foundations of Geographic Information Systems (GIS)Course completion shared publicly.
  • QGIS Certification (Modern GIS)Completion shared publicly.
  • Get Started with Android App Development (SkillUp EdTech)Shared as a new certificate.
  • Introduction to Software Engineering (IBM)Shared as a new certificate.
  • Introduction to Mobile App Development (IBM)Shared as a new certificate.

The most important outcome is not the badge itself. It is the ability to ask better questions, understand systems faster, and help business teams make clearer decisions.

For verified certificate links from my CV-based records, see Certifications and Learning Track (2024).

Why This Matters

  • Better ConversationsI can bridge business and technical language more clearly.
  • Better DecisionsData and process thinking improve day-to-day execution quality.
  • Better SupportBusinesses benefit when advice is grounded in both operations and systems understanding.
More credential notes

Written by Thomas Hayford.
